body: {"rationale":"Well-structured bead with clear file:line citations, numbered ACs, proper phase/area/kind/spec labels, explicit parent and no-deps statement, and a standalone description that contains no /tmp or chat references. Three issues reduce confidence in unattended execution: (1) AC 5 gates on specific Go test functions (TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource, TestInitRegistersSkills) that may not exist — this is a docs-only bead with no stated Go code changes, so an agent cannot know whether those tests are present and runnable; (2) the label spec:FEAT-006 appears with no corresponding mention in the description or acceptance criteria, leaving an agent unable to verify its relevance; (3) AC 4 contains a conditional prose obligation ('the bead notes state that and identify the authoritative source checked') that is ambiguous about what artifact the agent must produce and where.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Verify before dispatch that TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource and TestInitRegistersSkills exist in cli/cmd/...; if they do not, either add an AC that creates them or replace AC 5 with a test filter that is known to pass on a docs-only change (e.g., the broader './cmd/...' without a -run filter, or omit -run entirely).","Add one sentence to the description explaining what FEAT-006 governs and how it relates to this bead's scope, or remove the spec:FEAT-006 label if the connection is incidental.","Rewrite AC 4 to name a concrete deliverable: e.g., 'If docs are auto-generated, a comment in the PR body identifies the authoritative source file and confirms both source and generated copy were updated; if not generated, no additional artifact is required.'"],"waivers_applied":[]}
